standard: Soft knock. Deliberate. They're not in a hurry, which means this was planned.
Tentative. First visit, or they've been told to be quiet. Both are interesting.
Light touch. They're not sure they have the right address.
That softness is calculated. They want to seem less threatening than they feel.
Restrained. Either polite or practised. Practised, I think.
Quiet approach, soft knock. Someone with a plan.
A soft knock from someone who knows exactly how hard they could have knocked.
The knock of a person who expects to be made to wait.
Careful. They've been asked to wait before.
Soft. They're hoping to catch someone off guard.
repeated: Second soft knock. They're counting the seconds. So am I.
Still soft. Patience or calculation — revising toward calculation.
Third knock. They've done this before.
The softness is a choice now, not a default.
They won't escalate. This is someone who's decided how this goes.
I have a theory about this person. It's becoming a conviction.
Still knocking softly. That's almost impressive.
The consistency is the tell. They're not nervous. They're certain.
They've knocked on more doors than they'd like to admit. I'm certain of it.
Starting fresh. Everything I thought I knew about this person is being revised.

standard: Loud. They're not hiding anything. Or they want you to think that.
Confident knock. Someone who expects the door to open.
That volume says urgency. Or entitlement. Possibly both.
Loud. They've been here before, and it went well, and they're back.
That was assertive. Someone who's comfortable taking up space.
A knock that says 'I could have used a key.' They don't have a key.
Firm. They know what they want. Or think they do.
Big knock. Either late, or the sort who assumes everyone is ready.
Forceful. They wanted to be heard. Message received.
Loud knock. Someone with somewhere else to be.
repeated: Second knock. Louder. They've decided patience has a deadline.
Still knocking. The deadline is approaching.
Third knock. This is no longer a request.
They're escalating. I've seen this. It ends one of two ways.
Four loud knocks. Either furious or very cold.
I've revised my estimate of their patience down sharply.
Whatever they came for, they really came for it.
The rhythm is getting irregular. That's an emotion, not a strategy.
I had a theory. The theory is dissolving under the data.
However this ends, I'll remember the knock. Always the knock.

standard: A pattern. They're announcing themselves. Triangulating now.
Coded knock. Either arranged in advance, or they think it was.
A sequence. Signal or habit. Possibly both.
Someone who's thought about how they knock. That's a type.
That rhythm. I've heard it before. I think I've heard it before.
A pattern knock is a social contract. They're offering a handshake.
They taught themselves that knock. Or someone taught them. I want to know which.
Intentional intervals. Building a profile.
A sequence this specific is either playful or urgent. Rate suggests playful.
They consider this place somewhere they're expected.
repeated: Same pattern. Committed to the signal.
Still the same one. Whatever they were taught, it stuck.
Third time. The pattern is now a ritual.
Some people never vary the knock. This is one of those people.
Four times, same rhythm. This person likes things to be as agreed.
The pattern is the message. Decoded it: they'd very much like to come in.
I've memorised it now. Against my will, almost.
Same knock, same hope. I find that touching.
At some point a pattern stops being a knock and starts being a statement.
I know this knock the way I know the postie. Completely, and with no idea who it is.

standard: Gentle open. Someone who thinks of doors as things that break.
Careful entry. Considerate, or carrying something. Checking for bags.
A slow open. They're looking before going through. Smart.
Measured entry. This person pauses at thresholds.
That care suggests they grew up somewhere where doors mattered.
Soft open. They don't want to make an entrance. Which is itself an entrance.
They're savouring it. Or checking. Either way, deliberate.
Careful open. Someone who considers themselves a guest, even here.
Gentle. They've been let down by doors before.
I can work with gentle.
repeated: Back again. The gentleness is consistent. This is character, not caution.
Second gentle open. A picture is forming. It's a good picture.
Still gentle. This is who they are everywhere.
In and out, always careful. Same with cupboards, I'd guess.
Back again. I had a theory they wouldn't return. Theory retired.
I've decided it's respect. Choosing to believe that.
Another open. Another careful one. I'm going to miss them when this ends.
Multiple transits, all measured. Fully invested in this person now.
They're going to close it carefully too. Already know it.
Consistent across every single threshold. Remarkable, really.

standard: Forceful entry. Late, or sees doors as obstacles. Late, I think.
Firm open. Either frustrated or just built that way.
Force like that suggests they've never been the one who repainted the wall.
Someone who considers doors solved problems.
Unsubtle. Interesting for a first impression.
Pushed through hard. Makes decisions quickly, adjusts never.
They had somewhere to be two minutes ago.
That was abrupt. They didn't look first. Confident or oblivious. Finding out.
Either angry or it's just how they move through the world.
The hinges are noting it. I am noting it. We compare notes.
repeated: Still opening like that. Not frustration — just their nature.
Every time with the force. Model updated.
This is how they arrive. Not going to change.
Third time, same force. This is now known information.
I've stopped being surprised. That's growth, probably.
Consistent force. Same with cupboards, I'm certain.
Always the same entrance. Always the same announcement.
Decisive every time. They move like the world owes them passage.
I've built an entire theory of this person from their grip. I believe it completely.
Every arrival: a statement. Every exit: I'll find out shortly.

standard: Gentle close. They thought about the door on the way out. Good sign.
Soft close. Either considerate or distracted by something much bigger.
They pulled it closed. Deliberately. Reading into that.
A quiet close. They're the sort who closes drawers properly too.
Careful close. Whoever they are, they got what they came for.
Soft. They're in no hurry now. Interesting.
They'll be back. Guessing.
They left quietly. Either something went well or something went very wrong.
A gentle close from someone who thinks the door has feelings.
Came carefully. Left carefully. Consistent profile.
repeated: Back, and leaving again, gently. Invested in this pattern now.
Second close, still gentle. Theory updating with every transit.
In and out, careful each time. Very busy or very restless. Possibly both.
Always carefully. I've decided they're a doctor.
Still closing softly. Still not telling me what they're doing. Fair.
Another quiet exit. Tracking open-to-close ratios. Professionally.
Back and forth with the same care. A lot on, this person.
Careful every time. Whatever they're carrying, it's invisible.
Multiple transits, identical gentleness. Discipline, not distraction.
I've built a comprehensive theory of their afternoon. It may be entirely wrong.

standard: A slam. Something happened. Something happened before they got here.
Hard close. They had a feeling they needed to exit.
That was a punctuation mark. The sentence before it was something.
Either anger, or emphasis, or a very strong opinion about doors.
Already speculating about the conversation that preceded this.
That force. They'll have opinions about this later.
The door was the last available surface for that feeling.
A slam tells you something. Listening to what it tells me.
They didn't mean to, or they absolutely did. No middle ground.
Five theories. Keeping all of them.
repeated: Second slam. The feeling hasn't passed.
Still slamming. A dedicated state of mind.
Third time. Whatever this is, it's specific.
The slams are clustering. Narrowing the theories.
This is now a message, not an accident.
Multiple slams. Settled on one theory. Not sharing it.
Every slam a sentence. Reading the whole paragraph.
This is a chapter, not a moment.
My theory is getting detailed.
I know exactly what this is. I've been a door long enough.

standard: A hand. They've stopped to think. Or to remember.
Gentle touch. Saying something they can't put into words.
A pause. They needed a surface. I'll be the surface.
Light touch. Checking if I'm real, or telling me something.
They put their hand there. That's not nothing. Never nothing.
A gentle touch from someone who touches things gently. Good people, usually.
They stopped. Mid-thought. Hand found the door.
Light contact. Speculating about what they just decided.
A touch like that is either goodbye or hello. Maybe both.
Someone processing something. I'm the processing surface today.
repeated: Still in contact. The thought is taking longer.
Extended touch. Whatever they're working through, it's complex.
Still there. They're making a decision.
Long contact now. I have a full theory of what that decision is.
Whatever it is, it's large.
I find I don't want to rush this.
This is the kind of contact that gets remembered.
Fully invested in the outcome.
I have a theory. Rooting for the theory.
They'll carry this when they leave. So will I.

standard: Rough touch. Frustrated, or in a hurry. Frustrated about the hurry, I think.
Firm contact. Needed something solid. Found it.
Something is wrong, or something was just resolved.
This person uses their hands when they need to get through something.
A tell. Reading it.
Not here to be gentle right now.
Somewhere difficult, just arrived here.
They had somewhere else to be in their head.
Muscle memory for something. Want to know what.
Going to spend time on this one.
repeated: Still rough. Not passing.
Second rough contact. Pattern forming.
I've revised my initial theory twice already.
This is just how they are right now.
This is not about the door.
The door is incidental to this feeling.
Whatever this is, it's got weight.
A person working something out physically.
I have a complete theory. It fits everything.
Stopped counting. Started witnessing.

standard: A lean. They needed a moment. Providing the moment.
Weight against the door. Either thinking, or recovering. Checking which.
Resting here. Either they're comfortable or they've stopped being able to walk. First option.
Someone who needed to stop and found they'd stopped here.
They're deciding something. Or recovering. Deciding, I think.
Deliberate or involuntary — it matters which. Working on it.
Weight against the frame. They chose this spot. Interesting choice.
Comfortable here, or exhausted. Possibly both.
They stopped here. Of all the places to stop.
Going to have thoughts about this for a while.
repeated: Still leaning. Whatever it is — still in progress.
Extended lean. Moved from observation to investment.
Still there. Building a whole theory of their afternoon.
Long lean now. Something difficult brought them here.
Decided it's not entirely bad, whatever it is.
They could move. Choosing not to. Interesting.
Revised my theory. More complicated than I thought.
Committed to this. Whatever comes next, I'm ready.
Long lean. Long story. I can wait.
They'll push off eventually. They'll carry whatever this was. So will I.

standard: The flap. Something arrived. Everything depends on what arrived.
Post. The postie, almost certainly, unless someone's being clever.
Whatever it is, it was important enough to carry here.
The question is what the flap let through.
Someone decided this address was the right one. Interesting judgment.
Already speculating about the sender.
The postie's route is fixed. This door is not an accident on it.
Every delivery is a communication. Reading between the lines.
Something passed through. I have theories.
The world remembered this address. The world reaches out.
repeated: Second delivery. Busy postie, or an unusual day.
Still delivering. Either a full route or a very particular sender.
Third flap. Something is being communicated.
Either very ordered or slightly chaotic out there.
This is a significant day for this address.
The postie is committed. I respect that kind of commitment.
I've developed a theory about what's being delivered.
Something is happening in the outside world.
Fully speculating about the contents now.
The postie knows something I don't. They always do.
